name: Choi 2019 Tadalafil ED Depression Pilot Extract
population_or_context: Ten men with at least 3 months of ED and PHQ-9 score at least 5.
methods: Prospective open-label single-arm tadalafil 5 mg once-daily pilot study with PHQ-9, PHQ-15, and serum BDNF before/after 8 weeks.
population_profile: Men with ED and baseline depressive symptoms; indirect for healthy men age 30 to 50 without ED or depression.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily for 8 weeks.
indication_context: ED treatment plus exploratory depression outcome; off-label as an antidepressant interpretation.
study_design: Open-label single-arm pilot study without placebo or active comparator.
comparator_context: Within-person baseline comparison only; no placebo control.
safety_findings: Abstract does not report detailed adverse-event extraction; safety must be interpreted through the published tadalafil safety baseline.
directness_to_target_population: Very indirect: small ED/depression clinical sample, no healthy participants, no control group.
confounding_and_bias_limits: No comparator, n=10, regression to the mean and sexual-function-mediated mood changes cannot be separated.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil exposure; no cross-agent transfer, but causal transfer to mood benefit is weak because the study was uncontrolled and ED-specific.
